questions.question: Explain the four quadrants of the Cartesian plane with their coordinate signs.
questions.answer: The Cartesian plane is divided into four quadrants by the x-axis and y-axis. Quadrant I has (+,+), Quadrant II has (-,+), Quadrant III has (-,-) and Quadrant IV has (+,-).

questions.question: Explain how to locate a point (3,4) on the Cartesian plane.
questions.answer: Start from the origin, move 3 units to the right along the x-axis and then move 4 units upward parallel to the y-axis to locate the point.

questions.question: Explain how to determine the quadrant of a given point.
questions.answer: Check the sign of the coordinates. (+,+) → QI, (-,+) → QII, (-,-) → QIII, (+,-) → QIV.

questions.question: Explain why the point (0,7) lies on the y-axis.
questions.answer: Points on the y-axis have x-coordinate equal to zero. Since x = 0, the point lies on the y-axis.

questions.question: Explain why the point (6,0) lies on the x-axis.
questions.answer: Points on the x-axis have y-coordinate equal to zero. Since y = 0, the point lies on the x-axis.

questions.question: Explain abscissa and ordinate with an example.
questions.answer: Abscissa is the x-coordinate and ordinate is the y-coordinate. Example: For (5,3), abscissa = 5 and ordinate = 3.

questions.question: Explain why points on axes do not belong to any quadrant.
questions.answer: Points on axes have either x = 0 or y = 0 and lie exactly on the boundary between quadrants.

questions.question: Explain the importance of the origin in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: The origin (0,0) is the reference point used to measure horizontal and vertical distances in the coordinate plane.

questions.question: Explain how the Cartesian plane helps in graphing.
questions.answer: It allows points to be plotted using coordinates, enabling the graphical representation of shapes and relationships.

questions.question: Explain how the axes divide the plane into quadrants.
questions.answer: The x-axis and y-axis intersect at the origin and divide the plane into four regions called quadrants.

questions.question: Describe the position of point (4,-5).
questions.answer: Since x is positive and y is negative, the point lies in the fourth quadrant.

questions.question: Describe the position of point (-3,6).
questions.answer: Since x is negative and y is positive, the point lies in the second quadrant.

questions.question: Explain why ordered pairs are important in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Ordered pairs uniquely identify the position of a point on the coordinate plane.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between algebra and geometry using coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Coordinate geometry uses algebraic equations to represent geometric figures.

questions.question: Explain the contribution of René Descartes to mathematics.
questions.answer: René Descartes introduced the Cartesian coordinate system linking algebra and geometry.

questions.question: Explain the concept of plotting points in the plane.
questions.answer: Plotting involves marking points based on their x and y coordinates.

questions.question: Explain the properties of points on the x-axis.
questions.answer: Points on the x-axis have y = 0.

questions.question: Explain the properties of points on the y-axis.
questions.answer: Points on the y-axis have x = 0.

questions.question: Explain how coordinate geometry is used in real life.
questions.answer: It is used in maps, GPS navigation, and computer graphics.

questions.question: Explain how graphs help visualize relationships between variables.
questions.answer: Graphs represent relationships between variables visually using plotted points.

questions.question: Explain the significance of coordinate axes.
questions.answer: They provide reference lines for measuring positions of points.

questions.question: Explain why (0,0) is unique in the coordinate plane.
questions.answer: It is the only point where both coordinates are zero.

questions.question: Explain the difference between axes and quadrants.
questions.answer: Axes are the reference lines while quadrants are the regions formed by them.

questions.question: Explain how coordinate geometry simplifies geometric problems.
questions.answer: It converts geometric problems into algebraic calculations.

questions.question: Explain the significance of ordered pairs in plotting graphs.
questions.answer: They precisely represent points which form graphs of equations.
